file-curator
  • Shim Notify Gear
  • View page source

Shim Notify Gear

The shim_notify.py script runs as the main entrypoint of the Flywheel gear. It performs Z-shim quality checks on Siemens MRI data and notifies relevant users via email.

Each email recipient entry should include:

[[recipients]]
from = "scanner-alerts@example.com"
to = "tech@example.com"
host = "smtp.example.com"

Workflow summary

  1. Extract the first DICOM file from the ZIP archive.

  2. Parse Siemens CSA headers to extract the Z-shim offset.

  3. Determine scanner type from StationName.

  4. Compare Z value to scanner-specific threshold.

  5. Notify recipients with pass/fail result.

  6. Optionally update Flywheel session metadata (if run within a gear context).

Source Code Reference

Shim QC Script

This script checks the Z-shim offset from a Siemens CSA DICOM header, maps the scanner serial number to a PRISMA label, and sends a notification email indicating whether the shim value meets the threshold.

For SMTP, UPMC and Pitt typically use Exchange Online Protection.

class shim_notify.Curator(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: FileCurator

Flywheel Curator class to run shim QC within Flywheel gear.

reporter

Optional placeholder for future reporting extension.

Type:

Any

curate_file(file_: Dict[str, Any])[source]

Process a file by performing shim QC and sending notifications.

Parameters:

file (dict) -- Metadata for the input file.

shim_notify.first_dicom_from_zip(zfname: str) → Dataset[source]

Extract and return the first valid DICOM file from a ZIP archive.

Parameters:

zfname (str) -- Path to ZIP file.

Returns:

First readable DICOM dataset found in the ZIP.

Return type:

pydicom.Dataset

shim_notify.main(zip_path: str, email_configs: list[dict])[source]

Run shim QC check and send email notifications.

Parameters:

  • zip_path (str) -- Path to ZIP file containing DICOMs.

  • email_configs (list of dict) -- Email configuration dictionary list.

shim_notify.notify_message(scanner: str, z: float) → str[source]

Generate a notification message based on the Z-shim value.

Parameters:

  • scanner (str) -- Scanner label (e.g. "PRISMA1").

  • z (float) -- Z-shim offset value.

Returns:

Message indicating if shim is OK or bad.

Return type:

str

shim_notify.read_emails(toml_path: str) → list[dict][source]

Load email recipient configuration from TOML file.

Parameters:

toml_path (str) -- Path to the TOML config file.

Returns:

Parsed recipient configuration.

Return type:

list of dict

shim_notify.read_z(dcm: Dataset) → float[source]

Read the Z-shim offset from the Siemens CSA header.

Parameters:

dcm (pydicom.Dataset) -- Parsed DICOM dataset.

Returns:

Extracted Z-shim offset value.

Return type:

float

shim_notify.send_email(subject: str, body: str, sender: str, recipient: str, host: str = 'localhost')[source]

Send an email notification.

Parameters:

  • subject (str) -- Email subject.

  • body (str) -- Email body.

  • sender (str) -- From address.

  • recipient (str) -- To address.

  • host (str, optional) -- SMTP host server (default is localhost).

shim_notify.station_to_name(station_id: str) → str[source]

Map a scanner StationName ID to a human-readable PRISMA label.

Parameters:

station_id (str) -- The scanner's StationName from DICOM headers.

Returns:

Human-readable PRISMA label or original station ID if unknown.

Return type:

str

shim_notify.update_db(fw, dest_id, z: float)[source]

Update Flywheel session info with Z-shim value.

Parameters:

  • fw (flywheel.Client) -- Flywheel client instance.

  • dest_id (str) -- Destination container ID (usually an analysis or acquisition).

  • z (float) -- Z-shim offset value to store.
